content.problemStatement: Write a C program that takes two numbers and an operator (+, -, *, /) as input and performs the corresponding operation using a switch statement. Print the result upto 2 decimal places.
constraints: Division by zero will never occur
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two space-separated numbers num1 and num2. The second line contains a single character operator which is one of '+', '-', '*', '/'
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the result of applying the operator to num1 and num2, rounded to exactly two decimal places

sampleTestCases.explanation: Only one test case. 1 + 2 = 3, printed with two decimal places.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by using a switch statement to determine which operation to perform based on the operator. The result is then printed to the console with two decimal places.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total
